Fact: Aerodactyl shares its category with Arctovish, Arctozolt, Dracovish, and Dracozolt. They are all known as the Fossil Pokémon.
Aerodactyl is the only Fossil Pokémon capable of Mega Evolution.

Prior to Generation VIII, it was also the only Fossil Pokémon obtainable in any way other than reviving its fossil, breeding, or via the Dream World, as it was available through an in-game trade in Generation II, as a gift in Generation VII, and was one of Hayley's trades.
Mega Aerodactyl has the highest base Speed stat of all Rock-type Pokémon.
Aerodactyl is the only Pokémon owned by Lance and Clair in the core series who is not a Dragon-type Pokémon nor a Pokémon belonging to the Dragon Egg Group.

This is likely due to pterosaurs being known as "winged dragons" (翼竜 yokuryū) in Japanese, further explaining why it is called a "dragon" by an old man in Pewter Museum of Science in Generations I, III, and VII, as well as suggesting why its appearance is influenced by that of wyverns.
Aerodactyl was available through an in-game trade in Pokémon Gold, Silver, and Crystal, but not in HeartGold and SoulSilver, likely because the Old Amber could be obtained and revived in these games.
Aerodactyl is the first Fossil Pokémon to have its Egg shown in the animated series.
Aerodactyl is one of four non-Hoenn Dex Pokémon available in Pokémon Pinball: Ruby & Sapphire. The others are Chikorita, Cyndaquil, and Totodile.
In the Pokémon Red and Green beta, Aerodactyl was revived from a Wing Fossil instead of an Old Amber.
In the Pokémon Red and Blue beta, Aerodactyl was originally known as "Ptera", its Japanese name.

In the Pocket Monsters Encyclopedia, Aerodactyl was stated to be the predecessor of birds. This, alongside many facts in the book, would be retconned by future content in the games, specifically by the introduction of Archeops.
Aerodactyl was the inspiration for the naming of Aerodactylus, a genus of pterosaurs. The genus name was chosen in reference to Aerodactyl having traits derived from various pterosaur species.
